description: Canonical truth and artifacts require append-only immutability, while some runtime state files may need controlled overwrite.
preferred_resolution: Define artifact/event/history as immutable canonical objects. Define cache/state/temp/config snapshots as non-canonical mutable objects with versioning and audit trail.
owner_notes: Resolve by explicit object taxonomy: artifact vs state vs cache vs config.

description: Exploration-first can conflict with local goal-driven optimization used in operations.
preferred_resolution: Keep exploration-first at constitutional level. Allow goals only as bounded local optimization targets within L4.

description: Runtime loop and operational cycle are phrased differently and may appear inconsistent.
preferred_resolution: Treat L2 as canonical runtime loop and L4 as operational realization of the same loop.

description: Domain-specific rules may attempt to override global invariants or isolation guarantees.
preferred_resolution: All domain rules must be annex-level and cannot override L1-L3 invariants.

description: Core minimization may conflict with expanding operational features and tracks.
preferred_resolution: Keep core interfaces minimal; push feature growth to domains, modules, and external adapters.

description: Replayable state can be weakened by unstable external APIs or non-reproducible external data.
preferred_resolution: Persist external inputs, versions, timestamps, and normalization outputs required for replay.
owner_notes: Need ingestion policy and snapshot rule.

description: Auditability and monitoring can expose sensitive data if not redacted.
preferred_resolution: Require redaction and secret masking in logs, dashboards, and audit exports.

description: Parallel exploration and tracks can reduce stability or exhaust resources.
preferred_resolution: Govern concurrency with quotas, admission control, and rollback-safe scheduling.

description: Recovery actions may be implemented as overwrite or rollback of immutable artifacts.
preferred_resolution: Recovery must restore state by replay, pointer switch, or new artifact issuance, never by mutating canonical artifacts.

description: Some policy rules may be mistaken as governance or runtime rules during ingestion.
preferred_resolution: Classify by highest stable layer: invariants to L1, control to L3, operational process to L4, compliance/storage/security to L5.
